If you target an object, it must be able to fit entirely inside a 10-foot cube, and it can't be held or carried by an unwilling creature.

entries.name: Destination Requirements
entries: The destination you choose must be known to you, and it must be on the same plane of existence as you. Your familiarity with the destination determines whether you arrive there successfully. The DM rolls 1d100 and consults the table.

entries.name: Spell Description
entries: As you cast the spell, you draw a 10-foot-diameter circle on the ground inscribed with sigils that link your location to a permanent teleportation circle of your choice whose sigil sequence you know and that is on the same plane of existence as you. A shimmering portal opens within the circle you drew and remains open until the end of your next turn. Any creature that enters the portal instantly appears within 5 feet of the destination circle or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Higher Level
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the portal remains open for 10 minutes.

entries.name: Spell Description
entries: This spell creates a circular, horizontal plane of force, 3 feet in diameter and 1 inch thick, that floats 3 feet above the ground in an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within range. The disk remains for the duration, and can hold up to 500 pounds. If more weight is placed on it, the spell ends, and everything on the disk falls to the ground.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Higher Level
items: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the disk remains for 1 day.

entries.name: Casting
entries: Casting Time: 1 action
Range: 30 feet
Components: V
Duration: Up to 1 minute

entries.name: Effects
entries: You manifest a minor wonder, a sign of supernatural power, within range. You create one of the following magical effects within range:
• Your voice booms up to three times as loud as normal for 1 minute.
• You cause lamps to flicker, brighten, dim, or change color for 1 minute.
• You cause harmless tremors in the ground for 1 minute.
• You create an instantaneous sound that originates from a point of your choice within range, such as a rumble of thunder, the cry of a raven, or ominous whispers.
• You instantaneously cause an unlocked door or window to fly open or slam shut.
• You alter the appearance of your eyes for 1 minute.

entriesHigherLevel: If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have up to three of its 1-minute effects active at a time, and you can dismiss such an effect as an action.

entries.name: Thornwhip
entries: You create a long, vine-like whip covered in thorns that lashes out at your command toward a creature within 30 feet. Make a melee spell attack against the target. If the attack hits, the creature takes {@damage 1d6} piercing damage, and if the creature is Large or smaller, you pull the creature up to 10 feet closer to you.
This spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

components.m: the stem of a plant with thorns

entries.name: Thunderous Smite
entries: The first time you hit with a melee weapon attack during this spell's duration, your weapon rings with thunder that is audible within 300 feet of you, and the attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} thunder damage to the target. Additionally, if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet away from you and knocked prone.

entries.name: Tongues

components.m: a small doll of a ziggurat

entries: This spell grants the creature you touch the ability to understand any spoken language it hears. Moreover, when the target speaks, any creature that knows at least one language and can hear the target understands what it says.

items: Components: V, S, M (a sprinkle of holy water and diamonds worth at least 25,000 gp, which the spell consumes)

entries.name: Description
entries: You touch a creature that has been dead for no longer than 200 years and that died for any reason except old age. If the creature's soul is free and willing, the creature is restored to life with all its hit points.
This spell does all wounds, neutralizes any poison, cures all diseases, and lifts any curses affecting the creature when it died. The spell replaces damaged or missing organs and limbs.

The spell can even provide a new body if the original no longer exists, in which case you must speak the creature's name. The creature then appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 10 feet of you.

items: Components: V, S, M (an ointment for the eyes that costs 25 gp; is made from mushroom powder, saffron, and fat; and is consumed by the spell)

This spell gives the willing creature you touch the ability to see things as they actually are. For the duration, the creature has truesight, notices secret doors hidden by magic, and can see into the Ethereal Plane, up to a range of 120 feet.

entries.name: Wall Creation
entries: A wall of water springs into existence at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 300 feet long, 300 feet high, and 50 feet thick. The wall lasts for the duration.

entries.name: Unseen Servant

components.m: a piece of string and a bit of wood

entries: This spell creates an invisible, mindless, shapeless force that performs simple tasks at your command until the spell ends. The servant springs into existence in an unoccupied space on the ground within range. It has AC 10, 1 hit point, and a Strength of 2, and it can't attack. If it drops to 0 hit points, the spell ends.

Once on each of your turns as a bonus action, you can mentally command the servant to move up to 15 feet and interact with an object. The servant can perform simple tasks that a human servant could do, such as fetching things, cleaning, mending, folding clothes, lighting fires, serving food, and pouring wine. Once you give the command, the servant performs the task to the best of its ability until it completes the task, then waits for your next command.
If you command the servant to perform a task that would move it more than 60 feet away from you, the spell ends.

entries.name: Vampiric Touch
entries: The touch of your shadow-wreathed hand can siphon life force from others to heal your wounds. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target takes 3d6 necrotic damage, and you regain hit points equal to half the amount of necrotic damage dealt. Until the spell ends, you can make the attack again on each of your turns as an action.

entries.name: Spell Description
entries: You unleash a string of insults laced with subtle enchantments at a creature you can see within range. If the target can hear you (though it need not understand you), it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take {@damage 1d4} psychic damage and have disadvantage on the next attack it makes before the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Wall of Fire
entries: You create a wall of fire on a solid surface within range. You can make the wall up to 60 feet long, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick, or a ringed wall up to 20 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick. The wall is opaque and lasts for the duration. When the wall appears, each creature within its area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d8} fire damage, or half as much on a successful one. One side of the wall, selected by you when you cast this spell, deals {@damage 5d8} fire damage to each creature that ends its turn within 10 feet of that side or inside the wall. A creature takes the same damage when it enters the wall for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. The other side of the wall deals no damage. At higher levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.

entries.name: Wall of Force
entries: An invisible wall of force springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall appears in any orientation you choose, as a horizontal, vertical barrier, or at an angle. It can be free-floating or resting on a solid surface. You can form it into a hemispherical dome or a sphere with a radius of up to 10 feet, or you can shape a flat surface made up of ten 10-foot-by-10-foot panels. Each panel must be contiguous with another panel. In any form, the wall is 1/4 inch thick. It lasts for the duration. If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (your choice which side).

Nothing can physically pass through the wall. It is immune to all damage and cannot be dispelled by {@item Dispel Magic|PHB.Spell.DispelMagic}. A {@item Disintegrate|PHB.Spell.Disintegrate} spell destroys the wall instantly, however. The wall also extends into the Ethereal Plane, blocking ethereal travel through the wall.

items: Components: V, S, M (a small piece of quartz)

entries.text: You create a wall of ice on a solid surface within range. You can form it into a hemispherical dome or a sphere with a radius of up to 10 feet, or you can shape a flat surface made up of ten 10-foot-square panels. Each panel must be contiguous with another panel. In any form, the wall is 1 foot thick and lasts for the duration.

If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature within its area is pushed to one side of the wall and must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 10d6 cold damage, or half as much damage on a successful one.

The wall is an object that can be damaged and thus breached. It has AC 12 and 30 hit points per 10-foot section, and it is vulnerable to fire damage. Reducing a 10-foot section of wall to 0 hit points destroys it and leaves behind a sheet of frigid air in the space the wall occupied. A creature moving through the sheet of frigid air for the first time on a turn must make a Constitution saving throw. That creature takes 5d6 cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the damage the wall deals when it appears increases by 2d6, and the damage from passing through the sheet of frigid air increases by 1d6, for each slot level above 6th.

items: Components: V,S, M (a pair of platinum rings worth at least 50 gp each, which you and the target must wear for the duration)

entries.name: Description
entries: This spell wards a willing creature you touch and creates a mystical connection between you and the target until the spell ends. While the target is within 60 feet of you, it gains a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws, and it has resistance to all damage. Also, each time it takes damage, you take the same amount of damage.

entries.name: Spell Effects
entries: The spell ends if you drop to 0 hit points or if you and the target become separated by more than 60 feet. It also ends if the spell is cast again on either of the connected creatures. You can also dismiss the spell as an action.

entries.name: Spell Description
entries: This spell grants up to ten willing creatures you can see within range the ability to breathe underwater until the spell ends. Affected creatures also retain their normal mode of respiration.

entries.name: Waterwalk

components.m: a piece of cork

entries: This spell grants the ability to move across any liquid surface—such as water, acid, mud, snow, quicksand, or lava—as if it were harmless solid ground (creatures crossing molten lava can still take damage from the heat). Up to ten willing creatures you can see within range gain this ability for the duration.
If you target a creature submerged in a liquid, the spell carries the creature to the surface at a rate of 60 feet per round.

entries.name: WEB

components.m: a bit of spiderweb

duration: You conjure a mass of thick, sticky webbing at a point of your choice within range. The webs fill a 20-foot cube from that point for the duration. The webs are difficult terrain and lightly obscure their area.

If the webs aren't anchored between two solid masses (such as walls or trees) or layered across a floor, wall, or ceiling, the conjured web collapses on itself, and the spell ends at the start of your next turn. Webs layered over a flat surface have a depth of 5 feet.

Each creature that starts its turn in the webs or that enters them during its turn must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is restrained as long as it remains in the webs or until it breaks free.
A creature restrained by the webs can use its action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC. If it succeeds, it is no longer restrained.
The webs are flammable. Any 5-foot cube of webs exposed to fire burns away in 1 round, dealing 2d4 fire damage to any creature that starts its turn in the fire.

entries.name: Spell Description
entries: Drawing on the deepest fears of a group of creatures, you create illusory creatures in their minds, visible only to them. Each creature in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point of your choice within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature becomes frightened for the duration. The illusion calls on the creature's deepest fears, manifesting its worst nightmares as an implacable threat.

items: Components: V, S, M (fire and holy water)

entries.name: Effect
entries: You and up to ten willing creatures you can see within range assume a gaseous form for the duration, appearing as wisps of cloud. While in this cloud form, a creature has a flying speed of 300 feet and has resistance to damage from nonmagical weapons. The only actions a creature can take in this form are the Dash action or to revert to its normal form. Reverting takes 1 minute, during which time a creature is incapacitated and can't move. Until the spell ends, a creature can revert to cloud form, which also requires the 1-minute transformation.

If a creature is in cloud form and flying when the effect ends, the creature descends 60 feet per round for 1 minute until it lands, which it does safely. If it can't land after 1 minute, the creature falls the remaining distance.

items: A wall of strong wind rises from the ground at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 50 feet long, 15 feet high, and 1 foot thick. You can shape the wall in any way you choose so long as it makes one continuous path along the ground. The wall lasts for the duration.

entries.name: Casting
entries: Casting Time: 1 action
Range: Self
Components: V
Duration: Instantaneous

entries.name: Description
entries: Wish is the mightiest spell a mortal creature can cast. By simply speaking aloud, you can alter the very foundations of reality in accord with your desires.

items: The basic use of this spell is to duplicate any other spell of 8th level or lower. You don't need to meet any requirements in that spell, including costly components. The spell simply takes effect.
Alternatively, you can create one of the following effects of your choice:
You create one object of up to 25,000 gp in value that isn't a magic item. The object can be no more than 300 feet in any dimension, and it appears in an unoccupied space you can see on the ground.
You allow up to twenty creatures that you can see to regain all hit points, and you end all effects on them described in the greater restoration spell.
You grant up to ten creatures that you can see resistance to a damage type you choose.

You grant up to ten creatures you can see immunity to a single spell or other magical effect for 8 hours. For instance, you could make yourself and all your companions immune to a lich's life drain attack.

You undo a single recent event by forcing a reroll of any roll made within the last round (including your last turn). Reality reshapes itself to accommodate the new result. For example, a wish spell could undo an opponent's successful save, a foe's critical hit, or a friend's failed save. You can force the reroll to be made with advantage or disadvantage, and you can choose whether to use the reroll or the original roll.

You might be able to achieve something beyond the scope of the above examples. State your wish to the DM as precisely as possible. The DM has great latitude in ruling what occurs in such an instance; the greater the wish, the greater the likelihood that something goes wrong. This spell might simply fail, the effect you desire might only be partly achieved, or you might suffer some unforeseen consequence as a result of how you worded the wish. For example, wishing that a villain were dead might propel you forward in time to a period when that villain is no longer alive, effectively removing you from the game. Similarly, wishing for a legendary magic item or artifact might instantly transport you to the presence of the item's current owner.

conditionInflict: The stress of casting this spell to produce any effect other than duplicating another spell weakens you. After enduring that stress, each time you cast a spell until you finish a long rest, you take 1d10 necrotic damage per level of that spell. This damage can't be reduced or prevented in any way. In addition, your Strength drops to 3, if it isn't 3 or lower already, for 2d4 days. For each of those days that you spend resting and doing nothing more than light activity, your remaining recovery time decreases by 2 days. Finally, there is a 33 percent chance that you are unable to cast wish ever again if you suffer this stress.

entries.name: Description
entries: A beam of crackling, blue energy lances out toward a creature within range, forming a sustained arc of lightning between you and the target. Make a ranged spell attack against that creature. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} lightning damage, and on each of your turns for the duration, you can use your action to deal {@damage 1d6} lightning damage to the target automatically. The spell ends if you use your action to do anything else. The spell also ends if the target is ever outside the spell's range or if it has total cover from you.

entries.name: Word of Recall
entries: You and up to five willing creatures within 5 feet of you instantly teleport to a previously designated sanctuary. You and any creatures that teleport with you appear in the nearest unoccupied space to the spot you designated when you prepared your sanctuary (see below). If you cast this spell without first preparing a sanctuary, the spell has no effect.

You must designate a sanctuary by casting this spell within a location, such as a temple, dedicated to or strongly linked to your deity. If you attempt to cast the spell in this manner in an area that isn't dedicated to your deity, the spell has no effect.

entries.name: Effect
entries: The next time you hit with a melee weapon attack during this spell's duration, your attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} psychic damage. Additionally, if the target is a creature, it must make a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of you until the spell ends. As an action, the creature can make a Wisdom check against your spell save DC to steel its resolve and end this spell.

entries.name: Zone of Truth
entries: You create a magical zone that guards against deception in a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on a point of your choice within range. A creature that enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the creature can't speak a deliberate lie while in the radius. You know whether each creature succeeds or fails on its saving throw.

An affected creature is aware of the spell and can thus avoid answering questions to which it would normally respond with a lie. Such a creature can be evasive in its answers as long as it remains within the boundaries of the truth.

entries.name: Conditions
entries.description: Conditions alter a creature's capabilities in various ways and can arise from spells, class features, monster attacks, or other effects. Most conditions (e.g., blinded) are impairments, while some (e.g., invisible) are advantageous. A condition lasts until countered (e.g., prone is countered by standing up) or for a duration specified by its source. Multiple instances of the same condition on a creature have separate durations but do not stack effects. A creature either has a condition or does not.
entries.duration: Until countered or for a specified duration
entries.effects: Impair or enhance capabilities; non-stacking when multiple instances apply

entries.name: Blinded
entries.description: A blinded creature can't see and automatically fails any ability check that requires sight.
effects: The creature automatically fails any ability check that requires sight.
Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.
The creature's attack rolls have disadvantage.

entries.name: Charmed
entries.description: A charmed creature can't attack the charmer or target the charmer with harmful abilities or magical effects. The charmer has advantage on any ability check to interact socially with the creature.

entries.name: Deafen
entries: A deafened creature can't hear and automatically fails any ability check that requires hearing.

entries.name: Frightened
entries.description: A frightened creature has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of its fear is within line of sight.
effects: The creature can't willingly move closer to the source of its fear.

entries.name: Grappled
entries.description: A grappled creature's speed becomes 0, and it can't benefit from any bonus to its speed.
ending_conditions: The grappler is incapacitated (see the condition).
An effect removes the grappled creature from the reach of the grappler or grappling effect, such as when a creature is hurled away by the thunderwave spell.

entries.name: Incapacitated
entries.description: An incapacitated creature can't take actions or reactions.

entries.name: Invisible
entries.description: An invisible creature is impossible to see without the aid of magic or a special sense. For the purpose of hiding, the creature is heavily obscured. The creature's location can be detected by any noise it makes or any tracks it leaves.
effects: Attack rolls against the creature have disadvantage.
The creature's attack rolls have advantage.

entries.name: Paralyzed
entries: A paralyzed creature is {@condition incapacitated} and can't move or speak.
The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.
Any attack that hits the creature is a {@critical hit} if the attacker is within 5 feet of the creature.

entries.name: Petrified
entries.description: A petrified creature is transformed into a solid inanimate substance (usually stone), along with any nonmagical object it is wearing or carrying. Its weight increases by a factor of ten, and it ceases aging.
effects: The creature is incapacitated, can't move or speak, and is unaware of its surroundings.
Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.
The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
The creature has resistance to all damage.
The creature is immune to poison and disease, although a poison or disease already in its system is suspended, not neutralized.

entries.name: Exhaustion
entries.description: A special condition caused by environmental hazards or prolonged stress, measured in six levels. Effects can grant one or more levels of exhaustion as specified.

items: Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws

entries.text: If an already exhausted creature suffers another effect that causes exhaustion, its current level of exhaustion increases by the amount specified in the effect's description.

A creature suffers the effect of its current level of exhaustion as well as all lower levels. For example, a creature suffering level 2 exhaustion has its speed halved and has disadvantage on ability checks.
An effect that removes exhaustion reduces its level as specified in the effect's description, with all exhaustion effects ending if a creature's exhaustion level is reduced below 1.
Finishing a long rest reduces a creature's exhaustion level by 1, provided that the creature has also ingested some food and drink.

entities.name: Appendix B: Gods of the Multiverse

entries: Bahamut, the Platinum Dragon, is the god of justice and nobility. He is worshipped by metallic dragons and good-aligned dragonborn.

The D&D multiverse features diverse pantheons, with worlds like the Forgotten Realms and Greyhawk hosting expansive deities, while settings like Eberron and Dragonlance have more focused religious systems. Nonhuman races often share worship of deities across worlds, such as Moradin, who is venerated by dwarves in multiple settings.

Many deities in this section suggest the Life domain, particularly if they are closely associated with healing, protection, childbirth, nurturing, or fertility. The Life domain is broad, and any non-evil deity can choose it. Death domain deities are mostly evil, as detailed in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Most deities who choose this domain are evil NPCs, though players may worship a god of death with their Dungeon Master's approval.

The gods of Greyhawk come from at least four different pantheons, representing the faiths of the various ethnic groups that populated the continent of Oerik over the ages. As a result, there's a great deal of overlap in their portfolios: Pelor is the Flan god of the sun and Pholtus is the Oeridian sun god, for example.
Paladine and Mishakal are the deities who head the seven gods of good in Krynn.

The Sovereign Host is a pantheon of gods believed to govern all aspects of existence, while the Dark Six represent their corrupt, shadowy counterparts. The Church of the Silver Flame opposes evil but faces internal corruption. The Blood of Vol teaches divinity lies within mortals and venerates undead. Cults worship demons in Khyber, the Dragon Below. The Path of Light foresees a future where darkness becomes light. The Undying Court and Spirits of the Past are revered as ancestral spirits or undead heroes.
Bahamut, the dragon god of good, is revered by metallic dragons and good-aligned dragonborn.
Lathander, the god of life and light, is associated with the Road traveling into a sunrise.
Beory, goddess of nature.
God of rulers and guardians
goddess of offerings
fields.symbol: Sheaf of wheat with green ribbon

entries: The Norse pantheon includes two main families: the Aesir (gods of war and destiny) and the Vanir (gods of fertility and prosperity). These families were once enemies but are now allied against common threats like giants (including Surtur and Thrym). Deities like Frey (Vanir) and Odin (Aesir) share overlapping domains, such as association with the sun.
The Daghdha, god of wealth and crops
god of the sky, ruler of the gods
god of the sun, ruler of the gods

entries.symbol: face, rottíng on one side Woman's

fields.symbol: Watching blue eye,Rough ocean waves,greatsword Ice-blue,Head of a bearded man,face, rottíng on one side Woman's,scroll Winged,Flame,Gold coin,Solar disk,sword Upraísed,peak Mountain,sword Flaming,Hammer,White double-bladed axe,Sword,Longbow

entities.name: Appendix C: the Planes of Existence

entries.name: Material Plane
entries: The Material Plane is the mundane realm where Dungeon Masters run their adventures. It is the starting point for all adventures and the primary setting for most campaigns. Beyond it lie other planes of existence, including elemental domains, realms of thought and ethos, and the domains of demons, angels, and gods.

entries.name: Other Planes
entries: The planes of existence include domains of raw elemental matter and energy (such as the Elemental Planes), realms of pure thought and ethos (like the Ethereal Plane and Astral Plane), and the homes of demons, angels, and gods (including the Infernal Planes, Celestial Planes, and the Abyss).

entries.name: Plane Interaction
entries: Spells and magic items can draw energy from these planes, summon creatures, communicate with denizens, and allow travel. Examples include the Abyss (a plane of negative energy), Ysgard (a celestial plane), and other planes with unique properties like streets made of solid fire or battlefields where the fallen are resurrected with each dawn.

entries.name: Material Plane
entries: The Material Plane is the nexus where the philosophical and elemental forces that define the other planes collide in the jumbled existence of mortal life and mundane matter. All the worlds of D&D exist within the Material Plane, making it the starting point for most campaigns and adventures. The rest of the mult,iverse is defined in relation to the Material Plane.

The worlds of the Material Plane are infinitely diverse, reflecting the creative imagination of DMs and players. They include magic-wasted desert planets, island-dotted water worlds, worlds where magic combines with advanced technology, Stone Age worlds, and places where gods walk or have abandoned.

entries.name: Notable Campaign Settings
entries: Official published worlds include Greyhawk, Blackmoor, Dragonlance, the Forgotten Realms, Mystara, Birthright, Dark Sun, and Eberron. Each has its own heroic adventurers, villains, ancient ruins, artifacts, dungeons, and dragons.

entries.name: Feywild
entries.description: A parallel dimension occupying the same cosmological space as the Material Plane, characterized by eternal twilight, magical wonders, and realms of little people. Features include stationary suns, glowing crystal mountains, and territories ruled by the Seelie and Unseelie courts.
notable_features: Tangles of sharp-toothed brambles and syrupy fens
Domains of Seelie and Unseelie courts

entries.name: Shadowfell
entries.description: A darkly lighted dimension where color has been leached from everything, characterized by toxic darkness and a stark black-and-white aesthetic. Shares the same cosmological space as the Material Plane.
notable_features: Distorted reflections of Material Plane landscapes

entries.name: Positive Plane
entries.description: Like a dome above the other planes, the Positive Plane is the source of radiant energy and the raw life force that suffuses all living beings, from the puny to the sublime.

entries.name: Negative Plane
entries.description: The dark reflection [of the Positive Plane], the Negative Plane is the source of necrotic energy that destroys the living and animates the undead.

entries.text: Beyond the Material Plane, the various planes of existence are realms of myth and mystery. They're not simply other worlds, but different qualities of being, formed and governed by spiritual and elemental principles abstracted from the ordinary world.

entries.name: Planar Travel Methods
entries: Adventurers travel to other planes via spells or planar portals.

Spells: Plane Shift and Gate transport directly to any plane with varying precision. Etherealness allows entry to the Ethereal Plane and travel to adjacent planes (Shadowfell, Feywild, Elemental Planes). Astral Projection projects adventurers to the Astral Plane and then to the Outer Planes.

entries.name: Planar Portals
entries: Portals are stationary interplanar connections between specific locations on different planes. Examples include doorways, circles of standing stones, soaring towers, sailing ships, or entire towns existing in multiple planes simultaneously.
Some portals are vortices linking Elemental Planes to similar Material Plane locations: e.g., volcanic cores (Plane of Fire) or ocean depths (Plane of Water).

entries.name: Ethereal Plane
entries.description: A misty, fog-bound dimension sometimes described as a great ocean. Its shores, called the Border Ethereal, overlap the Material Plane and the Inner Planes. Every location on those planes has a corresponding location on the Ethereal Plane. Certain creatures can see into the Border Ethereal, and the {@spell see invisibility} and {@spell true seeing} spells grant that ability. Magical effects using force energy, such as {@spell forcecage} and {@spell wall of force}, extend from the Material Plane into the Border Ethereal. The depths of the plane, the Deep Ethereal, are a region of swirling mists and colorful fogs.

entries.name: Astral Plane
entries.description: The realm of thought and dream, where visitors travel as disembodied souls to reach the planes of the divine and demonic. It is a great, silvery sea, the same above and below, with swirling wisps of white and gray streaking among motes of light resembling distant stars. Erratic whirlpools of color flicker in midair like spinning coins. Occasional bits of solid matter can be found here, but most of the Astral Plane is an endless, open domain.

entries.name: Plane of Air
entries.description: The Plane of Air forms a ring around the Material Plane, resembling a world in the Material Plane near the Material Plane. Farther from the Material Plane, it consists of pure air.

entries.name: Plane of Earth
entries.description: The Plane of Earth forms a ring around the Material Plane, resembling a world in the Material Plane near the Material Plane. Farther from the Material Plane, it consists of pure solid earth.

entries.name: Plane of Fire
entries.description: The Plane of Fire forms a ring around the Material Plane, resembling a world in the Material Plane near the Material Plane. Farther from the Material Plane, it consists of blazing fire.

entries.name: Plane of Water
entries.description: The Plane of Water forms a ring around the Material Plane, resembling a world in the Material Plane near the Material Plane. Farther from the Material Plane, it consists of crystal-clear water.

entries.name: Elemental Chaos
entries.description: The Elemental Chaos is the farthest extent of the Inner Planes, where pure elements dissolve and bleed together into an unending tumult of clashing energies and colliding substance.

entries.name: Outer Planes Overview
entries: The Outer Planes are realms of thought and spirit, home to deities and the essence of alignments. They contrast with the Inner Planes, which are more physical and material. The Outer Planes are divided into Upper and Lower Planes based on alignment, with specific planes corresponding to each alignment and its shades.

The most well-known Outer Planes are a group of sixteen planes that correspond to the eight alignments (excluding neutrality) and the shades of distinction between them. These planes are divided into Upper and Lower Planes, with Upper Planes associated with lawful alignments and Lower Planes with chaotic alignments.

items: The Twin Paradises of Bytopia (NG, LG)
The Windswept Depths of Pandemonium (CN, CE)
The Tarterian Depths of Careeri (NE, CE)
The Bleak Eternity of Gehenna (NE, LE)
The Infinite Battlefield of Aeheron (LN, LE)
The Peaceable Kingdoms of Areadia (LN, LG)

entries.text: Existing somehow between or beyond the known planes of existence are a variety of other realms.

entries.name: Description
entries: The Outlands is a plane of neutrality situated between the Outer Planes. It exists in a paradoxical balance, incorporating elements of all planes while maintaining a unique equilibrium. The plane features varied terrain including prairies, mountains, and rivers, resembling an ordinary Material Plane.

entries.name: Structure
entries: The Outlands is circular, often likened to a great wheel. This structure is considered a microcosm of the Great Wheel cosmology, though its arrangement may have inspired the concept of the Great Wheel itself.

entries.name: Gate-Towns
entries: Sixteen gate-towns encircle the Outlands' perimeter, each built around a portal to an Outer Plane. These settlements share characteristics with the planes they connect to.

entries.name: Sigil
entries: At the center of the Outlands stands the Spire, from which the ring-shaped city of Sigil (the City of Doors) floats. Sigil is a bustling planar metropolis with countless portals to other planes and worlds.

entries.name: Economy
entries: Sigil functions as a trader's city, facilitating the exchange of goods, information, and portal keys. These keys are highly sought after for accessing specific planes or portals.

entries.name: Demiplane
entries: Demiplanes are small extradimensional spaces with their own unique rules. They are pieces of reality that do not seem to fit anywhere else.

They come into being by a variety of means: created by spells (such as {@spell demiplane|PHB2014}), generated by powerful deities or other forces, existing naturally as folds of reality pinched off from the multiverse, or as baby universes growing in power.

A given demiplane can be entered through a single point where it touches another plane. {@spell plane shift|PHB2014} theoretically allows travel to demiplanes, but requires a tuning fork frequency that is extremely hard to acquire. {@spell gate|PHB2014} is more reliable, assuming the caster knows of the demiplane.

entries.name: The Far Realm
entries: The Far Realm is beyond the known multiverse. It might be an entirely separate multiverse with its own physical and magical laws.
Stray energies from the Far Realm warp life and matter into alien shapes that defy ordinary geometry and biology when they leak onto other planes.

Entities in the Far Realm are so alien that they damage a normal mind. Titanic creatures swim through nothingness, preoccupied with madness. Unspeakable things whisper awful truths to those who dare listen.
For mortals, knowledge of the Far Realm is a triumph of mind over the boundaries of matter, space, and eventually sanity.

entries.name: Portals to the Far Realm
entries: No known portals to the Far Realm remain viable. Ancient elves once created a vast portal within Firestorm Peak, but their civilization collapsed in terror, and the portal's location is lost.
Other portals may still exist, marked by alien forces leaking through to corrupt the Material Plane.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: While it can't hear, the bat has no blindsight.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The bear has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bear makes two attacks: one Bite and one Claw.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the boar moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature right before hitting it with a tusk attack, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6+2} slashing damage and must succeed on a {@dc 11} Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

entries: The bear has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The cat has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The crocodile can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The wolf has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The frog can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: As part of its movement and without a running start, the frog can long jump up to 10 feet and high jump up to 5 feet.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The eagle has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The eagle makes two attacks, one with its beak and one with its talons.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The lion has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Pounce
entries: If the lion moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target right before hitting it with a claw attack, the target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the lion is prone, the lion can take a bonus action to make one bite attack against it.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The mastiff has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Beast of Burden
entries: The mule is considered to be a Large animal for the purpose of determining its carrying capacity.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The owl provokes no opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The panther has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d6+2} slashing damage.

trait.name: Keen Senses
entries: The pseudodragon has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight, hearing, or smell.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: The quasit can use its action to polymorph into the beast form of a bat, centipede, or locust, or into its demon form. Its statistics are the same in each form, although its attack is different in some of them. Any equipment it carries is not transformed. If slain, the quasit reverts to its demon form.

trait.name: Invisibility
entries: The quasit turns invisible until it attacks, uses Scare, or until its concentration ends. Anything the invisible quasit is carrying or wearing is invisible as long as it remains in contact with the quasit.

action.name: Claws (Bite in Beast Form)
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 5 (1d4+3)} piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or take 5 (2d4) poison damage and become poisoned for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect early on a success.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The rat has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The raven can mimic simple sounds it has heard, such as a person whispering, a baby crying, or an animal chittering. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful {@dc 10} Wisdom (Insight) check.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The shark has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the shark's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The shark can breathe only while underwater.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 1) piercing damage.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The tiger has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Pounce
entries: {@atk claw} +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d6+2} slashing damage. If the target is within 5 feet and the tiger moves at least 20 feet straight toward it, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the tiger can take a bonus action to make one bite attack against it.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the horse moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature right before hitting it with a hooves attack, the target must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the horse can take a bonus action to make another attack with its hooves against the target.
